triggers.action: Inject compact memory governance context and append a metadata trace.

triggers.condition: Stop at the end of a Codex turn or session
triggers.action: Write a reviewable memory candidate with metadata only.

triggers.condition: Task changed files, ran verification, shipped a feature, or produced a reusable workflow.
triggers.action: Use agent-memory-dream-loop to decide whether to record one memory candidate, eval, runbook note, or rejection.

triggers.condition: The user asks for /goal, persistent objectives, long-running work, SDD/OpenSpec execution, or to keep working until a verifiable goal is done.
triggers.action: Use goal-driven-workflow to generate a scoped goal contract with Done when, Stop if, and token budget before implementation.

triggers.condition: A build, test, audit, security check, deployment, or user acceptance loop failed more than once.
triggers.action: Capture the failure pattern as negative memory or an eval candidate after redaction.

triggers.condition: The user corrects a process, preference, standard, or expected output shape that will matter again.
triggers.action: Create a compact semantic or procedural memory candidate with source and scope.

triggers.condition: A new skill is proposed, a skill is updated, or duplicate/overlapping skills are detected.
triggers.action: Route through skill-evolution-loop and the lifecycle policy before any skill mutation.

runtime_fallbacks.preferred: rules wrapper plus implicit skill trigger
runtime_fallbacks.install: Use dev-tool-adapter to generate .cursor/rules from agent-memory-dream-loop.

runtime_fallbacks.preferred: rules wrapper plus implicit skill trigger
runtime_fallbacks.install: Use dev-tool-adapter to add memory trigger policy to .rules.

runtime_fallbacks.preferred: opencode.json guidance plus implicit skill trigger
runtime_fallbacks.install: Use dev-tool-adapter to map the fallback skill and control policy into .opencode/.

runtime_fallbacks.preferred: workspace or user skill plus implicit trigger
runtime_fallbacks.install: Install canonical skill folders to ~/.openclaw/skills or workspace skills.
